codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit is small and targeted: it adds MarketVersionRegistry reads to the market snapshot loader and surfaces active/latest version cards in the read layer. That matches the missing VAL-CROSS-002 evidence path without regressing the existing canonical-manifest read model; manifest.ts already points at the canonical deployment, observer reads still go through LendingCore, and recent activity still has a live-or-snapshot fallback. I did not find any blocking issue introduced by this commit for the frontend-write-path milestone.

issues.description: The frontend still hard-codes a placeholder WalletConnect project ID (`DUALVM_LENDING_HACKATHON`), which the worker's browser check confirmed produces 400/403 console errors on page load. Read-mode behavior still works, so this does not block the milestone, but the feature's broader 'fix any console errors' wording is not fully achieved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill tells workers to validate with plain `npx vite build`, but this feature's worker had repeated OOM failures until switching to `NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=1024' npx vite build`. The skill should mention this memory-constrained-machine workaround so future workers do not rediscover it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:45-47 documents plain `npx vite build`; the handoff for this feature records multiple failed build attempts and final success only with `NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=1024'` plus a matching skillFeedback suggestion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not currently record that the checked-in frontend uses an intentional placeholder WalletConnect project ID, so validators/reviewers will keep re-investigating the same 400/403 console noise during browser checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dualvm/src/lib/wagmiConfig.ts:30-40 hard-codes `DUALVM_LENDING_HACKATHON`; the feature handoff's interactive checks explicitly note recurring WalletConnect/Reown 400/403 console errors; .factory/library/environment.md contains frontend manifest notes but no WalletConnect caveat.

summary: Pass. Commit 0875ea2 cleanly adds active/latest market version reads and UI display, which is enough to make the frontend reflect MarketVersionRegistry state while preserving the existing canonical-manifest read flows. The only issue I found is non-blocking console noise from the pre-existing placeholder WalletConnect project ID.
